fields.customfield_11403: The first part is about the EntitlementDetailChanged event, and the pearson courseware lambda is not handling that event type.

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product. In other words, a problem with production code.

fields.description:
When a student opts out and into a program or material, we need to ensure we are making the corresponding API calls to Pearson to help ensure appropriate material access for the student. 

Until this is resolve, we should run the opting script daily (or multiple times per day?)

fields.customfield_11310:
* Create the ability to set a default AccessMethod for courseware at the publisher (producer) level
* In the Create Organization page, add “Default Courseware Access Method” as a new field, just below Fulfillment Price Type. node_modules This field should be required when creating a new organization. node_modules Default it to CODED, and allow the user to either leave that or choose CODELESS instead. node_modules In the Organization Details page, add “Default Courseware Access Method” as a field, just below Fulfillment Price Type node_modules Backfill all existing publisher orgs (producers) with their correct defaults (Sara will get a list from Pub Team)

* Should we remove the duplicated data from Organization Program?

fields.customfield_11405: As a Publisher, I need to be able to set my default AccessMethod (Coded/Codeless) for all of my Access adoptions so that I don’t need to tell the platform what my preferred access method is for each term or campus or course.

fields.customfield_11310:
* Add an option to edit in the 3 dot menus in the price schedules table
* Allow users to choose that new edit option - they should land on the pricing schedule configuration page as if they’re adding a new schedule, but with the existing parameters filled in
* Add a Save Changes button
* Once the user clicks Save Changes, they should be prompted to recalculate
* All of the same conflict prevention should apply the same as adding new schedules
fields.customfield_11405: As a member of the Pub Team, I want to be able to edit existing Price Schedules so that I don’t have to completely delete and rebuild a schedule if I need to make a small change.
